title: astragalus sanguineus
intro: Astragalus sanguineus, also known as red milk vetch or red milk vetch Astragalus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body resist stress and promote overall well-being. The medicinal properties of Astragalus sanguineus are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, thereby conferring a range of health benefits, including enhanced immune function, reduced fatigue, and improved cardiovascular health. Astragalus sanguineus is typically prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to support overall health and resilience.
